comments.text: Got bored tonight, so I downloaded 3 hours worth of Earthcam replay / archive footage from their Skydeck camera atop of the Willis Tower (Sears Tower) in Chicago and made a time lapse of the fog rolling in off Lake Michigan. Good illustration of how our atmosphere behaves like any other fluid. #ilwx
Fog rolling in from Lake Michigan towards Wrigley tonight:
Loving the cloud pattern outlining the Great Lakes this afternoon.

🧵
This is a colossal roll cloud (Cloud Atlas name "volutus", which is a Latin term for rolled) over Lake Michigan. Video seen here➡️ youtube.com/watch?v=b_G7... (Turn on the sound) 🧪 #science #clouds #nature 1/3
The sea smoke on Lake Superior was amazing this morning. It took about 30 minutes before the sun got above it, but right after it broke the top of the smoke the world turned yellow.
Really wild and unique cloud coverage over Chicago and Lake Michigan this afternoon #ILWX

This is a cumulus cloud appreciation post... Cumulus clouds outline Lake Erie in the Northern United States. As Earth’s surface warms in the summer sun, thermals rise creating countless clouds while the relatively cooler waters of Lake Erie remain nearly cloudless. (1/3

Sea smoke fills the air above Lake Superior on a beautiful winter morning on Minnesota's North Shore. Thank you for taking a look and following along! Happy Sunday! #BlueSky #Minnesota #NorthShore #landscapephotography #minnesotanorthshore

A USAF F-22 Raptor flies near the sun creating some rainbow vapes at the Canadian International Air Show for its 75th anniversary year. The humidity of Lake Ontario does not disappoint for vapour! #avgeek #aviation

By the edge of Lake Michigan, everything disappears into the fog. The snow crunches underfoot, and the cold air feels heavy. Chicago winters are harsh, but there’s a strange calm in moments like this. #chicago

Cw: Lewd / Hyper Oh I just realized tomorrow is the end of NNN. So I'm expecting the world to smell like the inside of a condom with everyone releasing loads that could fill the great lakes. I mean it's already bright white outside my window. Snow, or can people barely contain their pent up selves?
mix of cumulus and stratocumulus clouds above Lake Michigan in Chicago where it just felt like i was getting slapped by the cold wind as i took photos of the sky 🌱

One of the coolest features of the huge Lake Ontario shelf cloud last weekend, was these tentacle formations on the base. These are caused from warm and cool air battling it out close to the surface of the water.

A fog coming off Lake Superior as we wait for the front runners for Grandma’s Marathon. This is as good a reason as any to miss #At40

there's been fog blowing in from lake michigan covering most of chicago today. the fog dissipates as it moves inland. /r/chicago now has two separate threads of suburbanites, who have apparently never heard of fog before (???), freaking out because the skyline is hidden behind mysterious white stuff

It's foggy tonight in old Racine, on the shores of Lake Michigan. But fog and calm mean I won't have to go to my tornado shelter in the basement, as I did each of the past three nights when the sirens went off.

A man named Scott Johnson captured a video of a rare "fog tsunami" rolling over Lake Michigan. He believes it was caused by cold air from a nearby thunderstorm hitting dense fog above the lake, pushing it into a huge wave-like formation.
Lake fog on a -20 degree day in Port Washington, Wisconsin. #photography #greatlakes #wisconsin
